meaning: Stop before entering the runway or a protected area and wait for further clearance.
simpleMeaning: Stop before the runway and wait.
portugueseMeaning: Parar antes da pista e aguardar autorização.
operationalMeaning: Hold short prevents runway incursions — you must stop at the holding point.
whoUsesIt: Ground and Tower controllers; pilots read back and stop at the line.
whenUsed: During taxi when crossing a runway is not yet authorized.
whyUsed: Runway incursions are a top safety risk — hold short is a critical barrier.

realExamples.text: Taxi via Romeo, hold short of runway one eight.
realExamples.speaker: Ground

realExamples.text: Cross runway three six, hold short of runway one eight.
realExamples.speaker: Ground

realExamples.text: Taxi via Romeo, hold short runway one eight, ANAC 123.
realExamples.speaker: Pilot

pilotResponses: Hold short runway one eight, ANAC 123.
Holding short runway three six, ANAC 123.
icaoQuestions: Why must you read back 'hold short' on every taxi clearance?
What do you do if you reach the holding point before a runway crossing clearance?
commonMistakes: Rolling past the hold line without a crossing clearance.
Reading back hold short but not stopping.
Confusing hold short with line up and wait.
pronunciationTips: HOLD SHORT — equal stress, crisp stop between words.
Always include the runway designation in the readback.

compareTerms.note: Line up and wait is on the runway; hold short is before entering.
Holding short is your report; hold short is the instruction.

didYouKnow: Many runway incursions start with an incomplete hold short readback.
captainStory: Ground at Guarulhos issued a complex taxi with two hold shorts — the FO read each runway back; Tower thanked us for the crisp calls.

references.title: ICAO Delta Ground Phraseology — Hold Short
